task.title: Configure Repository Credentials
task.shortdesc: Read this topic to understand how to configure the enterprise-hub.juniper.net repository credentials for Cloud-Native Router installation.

context.p: Use this procedure to configure your repository login credentials in your Cloud-Native Router Helm chart.
The Cloud-Native Router Helm chart uses your enterprise-hub.juniper.net credentials to pull images from the enterprise-hub.juniper.net repository.
The Cloud-Native Router Helm chart expects your credentials to be in a specific format. One way of ensuring your credentials are in the proper format is to use docker (or podman).

step.cmd: Install docker if you don't already have docker installed.
Log in to the Juniper Networks enterprise-hub.juniper.net repository.
step.info: docker login enterprise-hub.juniper.netEnter your enterprise-hub.juniper.net username and password (or personal access token) when prompted. Once you've logged in, your credentials are automatically stored in a default location that depends on your specific docker installation (for example, ~/.docker/config.json, /run/user/xxx/containers/auth.json for podman, ~/snap/docker/xxx/.docker/config.json for snap, and so on.)

step.cmd: Encode your credentials in base64 and store the resulting string.
step.info: ENCODED_CREDS=$(base64 -w 0 config.json)Take a look at the encoded credentials.echo $ENCODED_CREDS

step.cmd: Navigate to the Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_<release-number>/helmchart/jcnr directory. Replace the credentials placeholder in values.yaml with the encoded credentials.
step.info: The values.yaml file has a <base64-encoded-credential> credentials placeholder. Simply replace the placeholder with the encoded credentials.sed -i s/'<base64-encoded-credential>'/$ENCODED_CREDS/ values.yamlDouble check by searching for the encoded credentials in values.yaml.grep $ENCODED_CREDS values.yamlYou should see the encoded credentials.
